reviews.review_text:
i was very torn about this game at first when i played because i thought that it was just really clunky and complicated, but i kept trying and you know what? in the end, i think that i actually enjoyed this game.

its kind of like the forest but with more survival management and grinding, so if you don't like grinding don't even think about this game! also the combat feels really clunky, but again, its also pretty realistic, which in a way actually kind of makes this game even scarier than the forest.

the story is really good. i would recommend playing this game just to experience the story.

my only real complaint that i can think of off hand is the dlc. WHY is it soooooooo long?!?! and WHY is it such a grind fest. really the only boring part about the game.


all in all, minus the dlc, i think that this game is well worth a grab if you are into hardcore survival, and if you don't mind dying a few times before you get the hang of things. oh, and after playing this game i have come to the conclusion that i will NEVER be going to the amazon rainforest because of...        ...leeches. darn leeches that can latch onto you while you walk through vegetation. no thanks.

9/10 i recommend playing this game to experience the story, and if you REALLY like hard, hardcore survival.
the game mechanics are nice but i swear to god i'd have higher chances irl in a forest full of crazy cannibals and dangerous animals... it's so difficult and extremely punishing

Don't recommend this game if you are playing with friends. There is a COMMON glitch where if you try to save, your character will freeze and you are unable to save. You can only force quit the game which will cause the save to corrupt. When you are only allowed to have one save per campaign that means, you and your friends losing hours of progress and restarting from the very beginning. Looking at forums this problem has been reported years ago and still continues to this day.

Bought as a coop game, but too many bugs as a coop game. Host needs to make sure to grab the important story mission items, like the map, grappling hook, and so on. There can be host-client errors that make it so that no one gets the item if the host does not grab it first. Also, just too much going on in general with each thing. Had to restart so many times and things still mess up so didn't get extremely far in the first map.

This is probably the worst survival game I have ever played, I am honestly shocked to see so many positive reviews for a game which is pretty terrible. Where to even begin...

The hunger system is far too punishing, it's like you have the metabolism of the flash. You know how in real life how you eat a perfectly balanced diet of food every 4 minutes or you starve to death because you didn't quite eat enough fat? And you're thirsty constantly aren't you? Like even after you just had a drink, you're still thirsty? Don't worry though because you can just place 400 coconut halves on the floor and that will collect water for you (this is by FAR the most efficient way to collect water - so I hope you like having a messy base). 

Combat is red hot garbage, if you aren't using a bow then you've come to a gun fight with a toothpick. Does not matter what you are fighting, men, beasts, magical ghosts who appear - if you aren't shooting them in the head with a bow then you are going to get slapped about. 

Which I suppose brings us onto the injuries... wow, if you thought the rest of the game was bad then you're in for a real treat. Everything injures you in some way, hope you have the EXACT thing you need to cure it or you are going to slowly die, just kidding - you need two minimum because just one won't do it. Want to go swimming? You're going to get aids, run out of energy and pass out on the ground? You've got a worm. Ambushed by a big cat? Now you have 3 slashes on 3 limbs. Do you like leeches? Oh boy you are going to have more leeches than resident evil zero. That's not all of it either. 

The sanity system is just like having cancer. I cannot even begin to describe how bad it is. Everything destroys your sanity until magic ghost men appear and just murder you. 

The base building is pretty trash, other games have done it better. There's about 3 useful things, everything else is just a waste. Hope you have long sticks coming out of your ass because you're going to need 800.000 of them. 

The story is just the worst thing about the game, and that's saying something. Boring, predictable, emotionless. It wants you to care about someone who you have no reason to care about. It's made worse by the ear abusing voice acting (you NEED to go into the settings and turn the voice volume off) and forced walking sections. It's like someone decided that the game itself was not torture enough and decided to up the ante with the story. I got the "bad ending" and honestly I just could not care about even looking up what another ending would be or what you would need to do, just grateful to be finished with the game. 

There's plenty of other things I can't even be bothered to write about. This game is so clearly just copy paste trash  that was pumped out to make some quick cash.

I don't understand the hype around this game. the movements are stiff and the game is buggy.
And why is the guy so hungry? is this a survival game or an eating and drinking simulator? 
Drink 3 bowls of water back to back, eat 3 bananas in a row and consume 3 different cooked pieces of meat just for this fat ass to say he's hungry literally 2 minutes later. 
Got this garbage on sale and still wish I could get my money back

A survival game with a ton of progression. At first, you don't understand anything; even getting clean drinking water is hard, and feeding yourself is absolute torture, you always end up running on half health because you can't find proteins or fats. Little by little, you start to understand how to survive in the jungle, where to find food, and how to keep yourself free of leeches and worms. Having to constantly inspect your arms and legs to find out what is killing you is a crazy mechanic. 
The jungle is a hostile place that will try to crush your will to live, driving you crazy bit by bit or just killing you outright via a large feline or a simple spider bite. Sound is key, because if you pay attention, you can "sense" the dangers around you. 
It’s highly recommended to build multiple settlements as you progress; they don't all need to be fully loaded, sometimes just a bed on a tree platform is enough to keep moving forward. But you can't ignore your basic needs or your gear (going fists-first into the dense jungle is a mistake). It's a fantastic game to suffer through solo, but if you play it with friends, the experience is twice as good thanks to the laughs and the coordination.
The story isn't bad; I've played both parts, though I enjoyed the first one more since the sense of mystery and discovery in the first map felt much more well-rounded.

Absolutnie uwielbiam tą grę, pierwszy raz grałam w nią na komputerze mojego brata i była to miłość od pierwszego wejrzenia. Nie grywam w zbyt dużo gier, ale tą musiałam kupić. Szczerze, jest to taki zdecydowanie lepszy Minecraft (i do tego Polski POLSKA GUROMMM). Moja mama też ją polubiła pomimo tego, że w ogóle nie gra w gry. Jedyne zastrzeżenie które mam do Green Hell to brak możliwości zmiany płci postaci grywalnej. Rozumiem, że w trybie fabularnym Jake szuka Mii, ale czemu Mia nie może szukać Jake'a??? Czemu niezależnie od fabuły nie mogę w trybie jednoosobowym i wieloosobowym grać jako kobieta? Mam wrażenie, że przez ostatnią aktualizację z możliwością personalizacji budowanych w grze domków GameDev Creepy Jar pogodził się nieco z tym, że dla niektórych jest to cozy gierka. Czemu więc nie dodać opcji gry jako kobieta? Nie proszę o funkcję kompletnej personalizacji postaci (koloru oczu, włosów itp.) tylko po prostu o opcję gry jako Mia, której model jest już w grze

I absolutely love this game, I played it on my brother's PC for the first time and it was love at first sight. I don't play many games but I had to buy this one. Honestly, it's kind of like Minecraft but way better (and it's Polish too *patriotic Polish joke*). My mom liked it too, even though she doesn't play any games at all. The only complaint I have about Green Hell is the lack of the ability to change the gender of the playable character. I understand that in story mode, Jake is looking for Mia, but why can't Mia look for Jake??? Regardless of the story, why can't I play as a woman in single and multiplayer? I have the impression that due to the last update with the possibility of customizing the houses built in the game, Creepy Jar GameDev has come to terms with the fact that it is a cozy game for some. So why not add the option to play as a woman? I'm not asking for the function of complete character customization (eye color, hair color, etc.) but simply the option to play as Mia, whose model is already in the game

This game is very fun and realistic, brutal, hyper-realistic Amazonian survival game on PC. It distinguishes itself with deeply complex mechanics where managing macronutrients, tropical diseases, and psychological sanity is paramount. It is a highly rewarding experience for hardcore genre veterans, though its steep learning curve can be punishing.

Cannot learn to start a fire, the tutorial is not explaining how to do what it wants you to do. What's the point of a tutorial if its not going to teach you how to use the buttons or keypad to do what you need to do. Got very bored with it very quick. Not interesting or fun when you cannot figure out why the same actions on youtube videos are not working for you. Maybe a bug or something. Either way I'm just glad I brought for dirt cheap (90% off) otherwise I would call it a rip off. Do NOT get this game unless you want to invest in a lot of your time figuring out how to learn the controls etc. because the game does not teach you that..

do not play unless you like to watch a lot of yt videos this game does not explain much at all most the time your looking at a book trying to find out how to live and when you finally do you get attacked by everything known to man and boom now there are no more medical plants for bandages and you die to everything i loved this game for the first few hours then it kind of just screws you over so like i said do not play unless you watch a lot of yt vids and me personally do no9t recommend this game even for 3 bucks its literal hell and every second i want to die now and if you do play this game don't forget to save or you lose everything no auto saves might change my review after i restart because i am hard locked

damn this game really underrated, even tho it was difficult in the beginning because we have to search the recipe by itself mostly, really recommended for this game, hope i see a game like as good like this in the future, really love this game

Love this game! It's not easy, there aren't any real 'Cheats' to getting through it... It's got a really unique crafting system that I enjoy and the sanity level addition with hallucinations that can hurt you is a cool feature. The storyline and options for dialogue throughout the game make it engaging, and I am hooked on finding out more information about the plot.
Stopped playing at the tutorial, the tutorial is terrible and doesn't react when you press the buttons on screen. (Using a controller, so it says exactly the buttons you should press)

How can I NOT recommend this game?
As a fellow The forest and SOTF enjoyer this is a MUST get. It's really fun (especially with company) and I haven't even finished everything yet! My props to the devs. It can be a little clunky at times but not as much as other reviews might let on (at least in my experience). Yes, it's harder (and a bit grindier) than the forest but personally I loved that and the story is really, really cool. 

Yes, yes and yes.
Actual fun, well polished survival game with a great story. Great co op game to play with friend. 10/10
Once you get through the learning to stay alive part, the game is quite easy. Becomes more of a building game than survival once all basic needs a done.

BUY ONLY IF YOU PLAY SOLO OR DUO. do not buy if you plan to have more than 2 players. the game has a known issue of lag/de-sync and will ruin your experience. other than that this is one of the best true survival games around.

Unlike many survival titles that focus mainly on gathering resources and building bases, Green Hell makes survival itself the challenge. Managing hunger, thirst, infections, parasites, injuries, and even your character's mental health creates a constant sense of tension. The Amazon rainforest feels both beautiful and hostile, and every expedition away from camp can quickly become a struggle to stay alive.

The crafting system is intuitive, exploration is rewarding, and the game's attention to realism is impressive. Learning which plants are safe, how to treat wounds, and how to navigate the jungle without getting lost becomes a satisfying part of the experience.

The story mode adds purpose beyond simple survival, while the survival and challenge modes offer plenty of replayability. The co-op mode is also a great addition, making the harsh jungle experience more enjoyable with friends.

The game can be unforgiving, especially for newcomers, and some mechanics require patience to learn. However, if you're looking for a survival game that emphasizes realism, atmosphere, and genuine challenge, Green Hell is easy to recommend.

Recommended. A brutal but rewarding survival experience that stands out in a crowded genre.

the hunger system is far too punishing, it's like you have the metabolism ofthe flash. the tribes members watch you, they send scouts to watch you, and you better hope the scout attacks you, because if not, he'll go back to the tribe, tell them where you are, and now the tribe knows where you live.
10/10 very realistic

Way too ♥♥♥♥♥♥ save mechanic! I waste a ton of time to build a hut shelter since it needs whole 13 palm leaves, because why not?! And then sleeping doesn't automatically save the game, but I need to magically find a hidden prompt on top of the building to save the game?! Also way too unrealistic. The hidration system is absolute ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥! In reality a human can survive without water for 2-3 days, but in this game just 15 minutes and you are dead. Spider bite also takes hours, but here in 5 minutes you are dead. I don't seem to find Molinera in the start of the game too (after the tutorial)...
The biggest problem is the game is INSANELY RUSHED! I am not given the time to learn or do anything, as there are constant threats everywhere. Even in the first camp a fcking spider bites me when I am trying to chop a tree for leaves. Like give me a fcking break I am trying to survive here in the very first camp!
As I play even more ♥♥♥♥♥♥ stuff happens. I discover the map and head for the lambda coordinates on like 100 energy AND hidration. All of a sudden my hidration drops to around 20% and my characted needs a drink! His energy levels also drop to like 30% and he needs to sleep. Well how the fck can I progress in the game with such insanely fast deteriorating stats on NORMAL difficulty???

Can't recommend if you are playing coop as there is a bug that can happen at anytime that corrupts your save (you will not be able to move whilst the save is happening and the only choice you have is to quit without saving). If this happens your save is corrupted and if you load back in you will be on a large floating platform and since the game does not do backup saves you will loose all progress made in the game. There are some possible workarounds by trying to either manually create backups or recover them from Steam remote storage, but when that doesn't work, feels kinda bad when a fully released game can just tank your entire progress for no reason. And a good internet search will show you this bug has been around for a long time without being fixed and it will likely never be fixed.

Too bad, because the game itself is fun
Its difficult in the beginning and is DEFINITELY NOT for the feint-hearted but once you get used to how the world works and the survival mechanics, its a game that will hook you for life!
Game is really fun especially with friends and understanding is also not that difficult
can anyone gift my friend alex thegame cause he is so unbeleivably chudlke that he cant spend 50 cents on the game even for poor soal me.

Green Hell is a very enjoyable survival game with a good story. The main reason I enjoyed it so much was the multiplayer mode, which made the experience more engaging and fun with friends. The game offers solid survival mechanics, immersive gameplay, and impressive graphics. Overall, it's a great survival experience and definitely worth playing.

spawned in and went a few meters away? screwed.

ate some random crap you don't know? screwed.

Everything wants to screw you.

but that's the point of the game - learning.

Please finish the game, it smells like little peckers
1 Graphics are ♥♥♥♥
2 Multiplayer is so-so
3 Puma lion tiger panther ass
4 All saves deleted
very good game, son meme running and walking animation, getting brutally ripped apart by crocodiles and eaten by skinny cannibal figures
me and my friend played this for a couple of days, lost our minds, wanted to swallow an dart frog by the end of amazonia, overall great game
This game is a solid masterpiece. Great Storyline, great gameplay and deeply immersive.
Really recommend Green Hell if you're looking for a new challenging game. The hard mode really challenges you, it's a game like none-other and you can plow HOURS into it unknowingly.

Where do i even begin. I just spent an entire irl day trying to learn a game that's so unforgiving you'd need to spend weeks trying to master even just the basics. Normal mode is NOT "reccomended for all players" like they said it would be. If only there was at least an option to turn off all the different nutrient bs but it's either ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ hell or creative mode with no hunger at all. Can't even turn off expiring food. And every day is the same struggle all over again, seemingly impossible to not be stressed out constantly about 5 things at once ever. Not at all made for me and I'm definitely not the only one who thinks like that. Not at all recommended for someone who wants to play a game. This is not a game. Genuinely way worse than dark souls and I've played the entire trilogy. Frustration level of ds1-3 doesn't even come close to...this.
great story and will be fun with friends intresting game

I like the jungle
Plants cool
Soursops are yummy
Jaguar is scary
Monstera fruits are yummy too

8/10 nice game, value for money
Very good mechanics , realistic survival but it s a little hard to get the hang of it
I just love this game! Unlocked all the achievements and it was a journey to remember. Surprisingly, it feels nostalgic to see the screenshots of my game, as if I once lived in the Amazon myself.
Great dinamics, graphics, gameplay. I like it!

Beginner tip: Complete the tutorial. After that your on your own LMAO. Personally its a great game specially with friends. It has such nice details and niche mechanics that even after my initial playthrough me and friends can still uncover some new tricks. Would recommend 9/10
Excellent game. Best survival game to date. Very realistic.

May be one of the worst games i have ever played. awful crafting system with unrealistic food and water drain and then die in second before you can react.

straight up awful game

Amazing game overall

- great story
- combat doesn't feel amazing but its not the focus of the game so it hasn't affected my experience to much
- survival is extremely difficult your first few times but gets easier with each attempt so dont give up and don't be 
  afraid to start a new world if this one is at a point where it seems unsavable
One of my new favorite survival themed games with real survival mechanics, the story is good, you guys should play this game.
Hard asl but thats what makes it fun, very realistic too itslit.

One of those games I I find myself cursing at my screen every time I die but getting lost in it for hours at a time. I haven't been this pissed since I played a souls game, but in a good way. I know I could turn off a lot lost of the survival settings but where's the fun in that.

sebenarnya tamatma ini game tapi ending jelek kudapat sm anak anaka tawwa, ka deh di speedrun ki ini gameka saking bambangta mau tamat, adaji rencana mau dimaini ulang supaya dapat good ending tapi magerma saya, funfact juga disinimi lahir sebuah jamur legenda yang bernama "galihnus penisus besaranus"

Despite being abandoned in a state that is still quite buggy and a little incomplete (especially regarding the multiplayer experience), Green Hell has a lot of gameplay to offer. A spoiler-free playthrough of the main campaign is highly recommended, and has some great twists and reveals.

This game was a trip, it makes you think of things you normally wouldn't consider.. You will have to adapt to the harsh jungle or perish from your lack of motivation for survival.
But its more than just surviving.. its learning about things you never thought would happen in the first place.
Things you would never really consider in the first place. Only real warriors can finish this game. There is no peace in the jungle, remember that. Every bush, Every leaf.. Every noise makes me wonder if its danger or just a pesky mouse.. even the wind isn't safe sounding..

10/10. This game might make you tougher.

>Finally find plant to help kill the parasites I got from drinking unclean water.
>Accidentally click and eat raw fish instead.
> Get even more parasites.
>Now teetering on the brink of death at night while I desperately try and find more funky lil plants to help stop my poor guy from ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ himself to death.

It really does feel like I'm playing Project Zomboid without the isometric camera angle. 10/10 would recommend.
Really fun... please buy it's really cheap on sale. You can play with friends and I have friends so... but you can also play solo and it's a realistic survival game if you're into that!

Excelent game.Just 12h and im still trying hard to survive the basic dangers... I do recomend the game a lot.
I know it`s asking too much but It would be great if these things get better solved: 
1. Multiplayer game saves: I play with a friend that doesnt have the best pc so somethimes he chashes or internet loss and so he looses everithing just because the saving feuture has to be manually by going to base tent and press save. So for him it is not just a restart the game and continue, no it is a reastart an going back to the items and all states of the last saving. 
2. Optinal: the fighting controls feel a little bit slow and the aiming feature is not that easy.

Beautiful game full of the harshest survival lessons. The jungle is an unforgiving place and this game captures it perfectly. Worth sandboxing as well but the story loop is *chef's kiss*. If you like survival games, this is a hidden gem among them.

I bought this game in a sale for 2 dollars but I think it is worth the 20 dollars. I played this game for about 30 minutes and it already impressed me so much. The grafics, gameplay, the surviving in the jungle it is so good. Definitely worth buying.

Good game. It was really fun to play. The grind was worth it and I don't regret buying it. Really enjoyed the difficulty and had to adapt to the environment and stay alert all the time for snakes, scorpions, spiders and other predators. Early survival is difficult but you get used to it specially if you play on max difficulty where if you die you lose all your progress. Literally had to build a fortress just to survive on that difficulty. Hunting was also fun. Animals just run away after spotting you. Really enjoyed collecting animal and human trophies. It was really fun!!!

HOLLYYYY shizzle, this game I bought in the thoughts of 'hm cheap game, not much storage, should be fun and relaxing'

THIS GAME IS SO MUCH MORE, the accuracy is extremely good with plants, animals and their uses, the science is accurate to a point and even the bad ending was awesome to see. The impact of going from a basic survival to the story being so rich and tantalising in theme I BINGED this game,

It teaches so much in the way of botany and horticulture, definitely for plant lovers. 

If I had one criticism, its the 'unlock area' style of the game, each section presents its own faction of challenges and puzzles, but the idea of actually just roaming the amazon jungle would sound so much better to me. The last thing too, is this is a BIG walking simulator. like, lord of the rings style because if you run, you use valuable energy.

This game is super fun alone or with friends. The mechanics of the game are a little confusing at first, but setting your key binds to the way you prefer really helps the enjoyment and interaction of the game! My only thing I'm not to fond of this game is when breaking down logs for long sticks sometimes you miss and your axe still takes damage which in some ways i understand bringing out the irl in the game. But if I'm looking a log i should be able to hit the log. Only other thing is getting caught on things other then take so far with the 13 hours i have dumped into this game i really do enjoy it!

Few games have caught me by surprise like Green Hell.  The gameplay, graphics, and story were all fantastic.  The main thing to know here is the Spirits of Amazonia prequel is the beef of everything.  The main story took me 16 hours and the prequel(DLC) Spirits of Amazonia was 50 hours.  Pretty much 3 times the map and game time.  Factor that in with how you plan to play it.  Also don't be afraid to look up guides online as you will probably need them.  Fingers crossed for a Green Hell 2!

On m’avait dit que ce jeu était très réaliste sur la survie… Pourtant je n’ai toujours pas rencontré le moment où tu dois faire ta déclaration d’impôts ? On m’aurait menti ? 
Est-ce que j’ai d’ailleurs oublié de faire la mienne ? À cause de ce jeu ? 
Bien évidemment 

J’ai d’ailleurs réalisé que je savais mieux survivre face à un caïman sous folie meurtrière plutôt qu’à des vers un peu trop intrusifs à mon goût… (comme ma belle mère)
he game mechanics are nice but i swear to god i'd have higher chances irl in a forest full of crazy cannibals and dangerous animals... it's so difficult and extremely punishing
If you like Starving, Schizophrenia and eating maggots off of a dead animal this game is great for you

Yeah this is a hard one to evaluate. Did I have a lot of fun playing this game and getting my ass kicked with friends in the depths of the jungle? Sure, absolutely. The game has great mechanics and I especially enjoyed the nutrient management aspects. But there were so many bugs, glitches, crashes, and general issues with our cooperative playthrough that we eventually had to call it quits (one player could no longer move from a single spot on the map, regardless of whether the game was rebooted or not; another's entire inventory was wiped clean). So I guess the recommendation I'm giving is based on cooperative experience, and if you're getting this game for that, I'd try a more stable survivalcraft first.

This game is really accurate to real life. however, theres a weird amount of mushrooms that give you good side effects. its complicated, but its fun with friends! basically an evolved, better, more effective visual, complicated, version of minecraft! very cool.

Tried the game during the free week and ended up buying it right after.

I thought it was just gonna be another survival game where you build a hut and fight animals sometimes.

Instead I spent most of my time starving, infected, sleep deprived, and digging worms out of my arms.

Green Hell is honestly one of the most stressful survival games I’ve played, but that’s what makes it so good. The jungle actually feels dangerous. You can’t just ignore injuries or eat random stuff without consequences, and every small mistake somehow becomes a bigger problem later.

The survival mechanics are really solid too. Once you finally learn how to survive properly, it feels genuinely rewarding.

The story is also pretty easy to get into and does a good job keeping you interested without throwing a million confusing things at you.

10/10 - Great game, but the sound of bushes moving now activates my fight or flight response.
Really fun game. If you like survival games with complex mechanics this is for you.

Very very detailed game about Amazon jungles. Even though, it felt like watching a movie in cinema. I won't spoil too much but this game is definitely awesome playing with friends in multiplayer. Worth it!

I love survival horror, base building, open world. And this game has put me to the test. I really enjoyed the challenge. Story mode is fun, I was not expecting so much depth to this game. I came from Sons of the Forest and was expecting a "here, damn" experience, nope. Not with this game. It WILL challenge you, and it's a lot of fun.

It's a game that we can enjoy more in multiplayer than singleplayer. It's difficult in the begnning but it takes time to learn and make things happen quickly. So if anybody interested in way more difficult than any other survival like its feels very realistic and it represent real life difficulties through this game.

I like to play it with my friends but unfortunately i don't have friends with pc.

If anybody have friends don't miss this game it gives more safisfaction than any other survival game.

Unfortunately, I can't say with one way or the other if Green Hell is or is not worth playing or not this is due to the fact that the game refuses to either detect my controller or doesn't have any control compatibility at all. And I've tried ALL the troubleshooting options; putting Steam in The Big Picture Mode, unplugging and replugging my controller back in, and restarting my PC. Heck I did the last two things three different times and still the game would not detect my gamepad. So unless either the developers or somebody is able to assist with figuring out why my controller isn't being detected in game and resolving it, I'll have essentially just flushed $3 down the toilet. But that's no big whoop. What is kind of a big deal is but I also paid for StarRupture, and at the moment of writing this review I am downloading it, and I'm hoping like hell that game doesn't have the same issue with controller compatibility as Green Hell does...

Even 7 years later, Green Hell is still one of the better story survival games out there. The jungle feels brutal and immersive, with realistic survival mechanics. What really makes it stand out though is the story it gives you a real reason to keep pushing forward instead of just surviving for no reason lol
the game is a great game and its not just any survival game. You have to make ur own tools and build.

Game is pretty decent, I recommend it. Storyline also done very well. My only gripe with this game and hope it gets implemented at some point: for multiplayer, having the ability to adjust difficulty settings. The other thing is for the "dead / insta death zones" - there should be a 10 second warning if you're heading into one to give you a chance to get away. In some area's of the map, while water is easy to tell for most of the time with these area's, it's really difficult to tell what is a dead / insta death zone for the bordering area on land due to your render going past that and making you think you still have more of the map to explore in that direction.

ini game emang agak susah si tapi kalo udah ngerti mainnya seru banget haha,terus stroynya juga lumayan baguss

Got it on sale for 3$. Played ~50 hours. 
Very difficult at the beginning, but as time went on it only became better. 
Excellent survival mechanics. 
Thoroughly enjoyed it. 8/10
This game needs to be able to have a lockable door or hatch on your shelter to make it feel more secure. Other than that this game is an amazing adventure and is really fun to learn.
Really good ! Very difficult, especially in the beginning. Gotta learn all the plants and weapons and everything. After you learn the necessary, it's really good !
Pretty fun! Not as exciting as Green Hell VR but, it was still really fun. I enjoyed adventuring into the wilderness and exploring the map.
literally one of the best games i've ever played. like minecraft but im an adult now
Was given the achievement you're not ready got my ears blown out, crashed 10/10 would recommend the game.

The game has a very interesting concept, it really drives the point of how hard it is to survive in the jungle, and trust me, it is VERY easy to die in this game (which is a good thing). HOWEVER, my biggest critiscism comes down to the numerous and often game breaking bugs in the game. From things like sticks disappearing in the ground, to items phasing through the ground when chunks are unloaded and you lose them, to corrupt game saves. DONT EVEN GET ME STARTED ON MULTIPLAYER, its literally unplayable, randomly when players other than the host pickup items, it literally crashes their game (this appears like once per an hour of playtime). When they dont save their game, it resets their progress to their last save, so they need to save really often. When people load into the game and for example, they are on a treehouse, they will fall down through the floor upon loading, injuring themselves. Other than that, there is INSANE desynchronisation, items being visible only to certain players or just disappearing randomly (even for the host). EVEN GAMEPLAY ASPECTS, invisible wounds that are impossible to apply bandages to via the inspect option.
Overall, a very interesting game, i honestly bought it to play it with friends, but its impossible. Even solo is very annoying. Its so sad that the devs left it to rot and dont fix the bugs.
its good, but the injury mechanics can be insnely annoying sometimes, had to restrt due to a death loop

Story and atmosphere are genuinely interesting, but the physics killed my motivation fast. Something as simple as stepping over short grass requires jumping, and the jump mechanic itself is broken. Jumping in place without forward momentum, no directional input response while airborne, it just feels wrong. Got stuck on small patches of grass because the character refused to move through it, jumped in place, went nowhere. That's a UX failure that made me close the game within 20 minutes. Still recommending because the core survival concept is solid and the story seems promising, but the movement physics need a serious rework before this reaches its potential.
Realistic game, so complicated and can be tough, but so fun

I was stuck in the roof of a house because some big cats want to eat me and my friends rescue me. i spend 40 minutes on the roof crying for help.

Best game Ever
Great game. Probably the best survival mechanics out of any other game. But we need alive anacondas. That would be sick

My friend and I bought this game on sale because it was super cheap and we wanted a new survival game to play together. On our first day playing we both complained about how terrible the gameplay way, how unintuitive the controls were, we had to google stuff and so on. We spent hours playing and just complaining. Then we came back for another day. And another. 

We just finished the whole game PLUS the Spirits of Amazonia DLC. We freaking loved it. The story is cool, and while it does feel clunky and unnecessarily complicated in the beginning, once you get the hang of the nutrition macros it's actually pretty easy to play. Highly recommend if you and your mates need a new game to get into.
very tedious at first, but you get the hang of it fast and then its just strats to survive
the best story of any game i have ever played ,get through the learning curve that is super punishing and enjoy

The single-player campaign tells a genuinely great story wrapped inside a brutal, top-tier survival loop where every single ambient sound effect acts as a heart-stopping jump-scare. 

However, the true "hell" is the multiplayer desync. The co-op connection is so chaotic you will randomly teleport across the jungle and hallucinate entirely different realities - leaving you staring at a bowl of water while your friend swears it is a bowl of soup. 

It is a brilliant game that will test your survival skills, your sanity, and your friendship all at once.

If you think you’re good at survival games, Green Hell is here to humbly check your ego. This isn't just a crafting simulator; it's a brutal, psychological battle against the Amazon rainforest.

The game forces you to pay attention to details that others ignore. You aren't just managing a generic "hunger bar"—you have to balance macronutrients (proteins, carbs, fats, and hydration). You have to check your limbs for leeches, treat infected wounds properly, and guard your sanity, because the jungle will play tricks on your mind if you let it slip.

The environmental design is stunningly dense and claustrophobic. Every sound matters, from the rustle of a hidden snake to the distant calls of native tribes. It’s punishing, yes, but when you finally learn how to read the jungle, build a secure camp, and conquer the elements, the sense of accomplishment is unmatched.

An absolute must-play for hardcore survival enthusiasts. Just remember to check your boots.

Just finished the story mode. Awesome fragmented story telling and intriguing narrative. You have to constantly question what direction things are going and piece together the story as you play. Along with brutal survival gameplay. Got it on sale for $2 but I believe it is worth the full $25 price if you like hardcore survival.

Best game i've played in a while. Super challenging which makes it super fun
